Lead Applicant DetailsUnmet Patient NeedOsteoporosis is a debilitating disease characterised by loss of bone mass and deterioration of the bone structure,resulting in a significantly increased risk of fragility fractures. An estimated 17% of men and 34% of women over 50 are affected by osteoporosis worldwide[1], with resulting fragility fractures accounting for significant health and social care costs. In 2017, fragility fractures cost the UK £4. 5bn with an associated one-year mortality rate of 33% –and such fractures are set to rise by 27% by 2030[2]. Osteoporosis is highly treatable, but drastically under-detected. Often those at high risk of fracture are not identified or treated, with an estimated treatment gap of 66% for women in the UK[2]. IBEX Bone Health (BH)will accurately predict osteoporosis from wrist x-rays opportunistically taken using widely available equipment whenever patients are x-rayed for other indications. A data-audit at Truro showed that only 11. 8% of 2,719 wrist x-rays were referred for a costly DXA;

secrc__001_b.text: 23% had osteoporosis. IBEX BH would ensure the other 89% were analysed at very low cost. Areal Bone Mineral Density (aBMD), measured at the hips and lumbar spine by Dual Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DXA)is the gold standard biomarker for osteoporosis. But NHS osteoporosis treatment waiting times of up to 16 months are primarily caused by poor availability of DXA equipment[4]and specialist radiographers. IBEX BH is a patented software product that derives a quantitative measure of aBMD comparable with DXA, from a standard clinical radiograph of the wrist, due to be granted CE marking imminently. IBEX BH can utilise fast, low cost and widely accessible digital radiography (DR)capacity to measure bone health and fracture risk, leading to earlier treatment and fracture prevention when used opportunistically on radiographs taken for other indications.

secrc__001_d.text: Faster confirmation of imminent fracture risk, providing another, more rapid referral to treatmentReduced time to treatment for osteoporosis by triaging DXA referralsOpportunistic identification of osteoporosis from X-rays collected for other purposes, enabling referral to primary care and earlier treatment The primary route to market for the product is via integration with digital X-ray systems manufacturers who already sell into the NHS. The software package would be offered as an upgrade on installed systems or as an option on new systems being procured. This project will explore the feasibility of integrating IBEX BH outputs into existing NHScare pathways and will help to define a longitudinal study (targeting a i4i Product Development Award call)that will demonstrate value to the NHS. IBEX BH is based on a unique combination of physics modelling and AI methods, protected by several patents. The project will build on the existing IBEX IP and will not require licencing of third-party IP, other than commercially available software production tools. Any arising foreground IP will be governed by a collaboration agreement to define ownership. secrc__001_g.text: IBEX has developed a comprehensive health economics model, audited by York Healthcare Economics Consortium. If implemented across the NHS over 20 years, IBEX BH adoption is predicted to generate up to £2bn of cost savings as a result of 429,000 fewer fractures and 12,000 fewer deaths.  Additional service user benefits include: Earlier opportunistic detection of osteoporosis in over-50s –leading to reduced time to treatment and ultimately prevention of future fragility fractures. Increased access to diagnosis for users with learning difficulties or mobility issues for whom DXA is not possible –promoting equality of service provision. Past PPI involvement has identified general support for the rationale of IBEX BH, but stressed the importance of earnt trust via an evidence base[10]. This work flagged the importance of PPIE for the successful exploitation of IBEX BH, particularly in integrating how further treatment and/or testing is communicated with patients. A PPIE lead has been identified who will actively encourage a patient voice across the project’s duration. 8. secrc__001_l.text: 5. Further commercial development of the software (ongoing). Applicant and Team DetailsCompany InformationIBEX is focused on the development of medical X-ray imaging software to address population health challenges andimprove the value of radiography outputs. The Company has a strong track record of success in delivering grant-funded projects, including a large clinical study funded with a €1. 6m Horizon2020 project (ref 777835)and is currently focused on commercialising the IBEX Bone Health product via integration into OEM systems. IBEX has recently completed a self-funded study in collaboration with the University of Exeter to demonstrate the accuracy of the wrist-based product, and this project builds upon that research as a stepping stone to demonstration of clinical efficacy and deployment within the healthcare setting.  Whilst this project focuses on the clinical feasibility of deploying the wrist-based IBEX BH product for digital radiography systems, other work is ongoing to develop the technology for use on additional anatomies including knee, ankle and pelvis (funded through the Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst programme)and to facilitate deployment on mammography systems to allow bone health assessments to be made from wrist scans collected on mammography at the same time as screening appointments (funded by the Innovate UK SMART programme).

secdt__003.text:
IBEX will assign a project manager to oversee delivery of the project in line with project and risk management procedures outlined in the company’s ISO13485 accredited quality management system.
A financial package of up £149,492 has been costed. £22,792 will cover staff support for IBEX. £116,700 is allocated to UoE, who will be responsible for development and execution of the study. Their costs are split between staff costs and indirect and estate support. Up to £10,000 is allocated to PPIE activities (travel, subsistence and time reimbursement)and research team site and conference expenses (travel, accommodation, subsistence). The latter will be kept to a minimum with an awareness of sustainability.
External NHS Support &Treatment costs are estimated to be £4045.
i4i Connect funding will allow us to quickly validate the feasibility of suitable care pathway integrations which will culminate in the formulation of a comprehensive clinical trial design, anticipated to be supported by i4i PDA funding.
The project team has an established track record. Ibex Innovations and the University of Exeter (UoE)have worked collaboratively since 2020, in particular with successful Innovate UK funding for pre-clinical development of IBEX BH. A tripartite Memorandum of Understanding with Royal Cornwall Hospitals Trust (RCHT)has been in place since September 2023.
IBEX will provide a dedicated project lead for the duration of the project, along with technical support for the software product, and a permanently employed health statistician. IBEX also have expertise to develop the user experience and graphical user interface of IBEX BH and its outputs based on clinical and PPI feedback.
UoE will act as sponsor for the study, with Dr Robert Meertens the named principal investigator and project manager for the day-to-day running of the study. UoE has two further established academics linked to the project, with expertise in healthcare modelling (Dr Sebastian Rachuba)and health technology assessment (Zhivko Zhelev).
Dr Tom Bean (Musculoskeletal Radiologist)and Penny Lewis (DXA Service Lead)are RCHT clinical leads on the project. Funding is also requested for further clinical support of data collection from NIHR-funded research nurses, a UoE research radiographer, and research assistant from RCHT, with specific colleagues already identified for these potential roles.
Kath Maquire will act as PPIE lead for the full duration of the project, with existing links to the South West Peninsula Patient and Public Engagement Group (PenPEG)for workshop recruitment.
The project, and in particular the pilot study (work package 4)will have support from the South West NIHR Clinical Trials Unit, and project design has been supported by Imperial Research Support Service hub.
Regular monthly meetings with the aforementioned research team will be held to ensure work packages are executed to a pre-determined timeline using work packages as milestones.

evidence.text: 5. Further commercial development of the software (ongoing). Applicant and Team DetailsCompany InformationIBEX is focused on the development of medical X-ray imaging software to address population health challenges andimprove the value of radiography outputs. The Company has a strong track record of success in delivering grant-funded projects, including a large clinical study funded with a €1. 6m Horizon2020 project (ref 777835)and is currently focused on commercialising the IBEX Bone Health product via integration into OEM systems. IBEX has recently completed a self-funded study in collaboration with the University of Exeter to demonstrate the accuracy of the wrist-based product, and this project builds upon that research as a stepping stone to demonstration of clinical efficacy and deployment within the healthcare setting.  Whilst this project focuses on the clinical feasibility of deploying the wrist-based IBEX BH product for digital radiography systems, other work is ongoing to develop the technology for use on additional anatomies including knee, ankle and pelvis (funded through the Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst programme)and to facilitate deployment on mammography systems to allow bone health assessments to be made from wrist scans collected on mammography at the same time as screening appointments (funded by the Innovate UK SMART programme).

Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: Prior grants (Horizon2020, Innovate UK) demonstrate funding success; health economics model predicts substantial societal impact (e.g., 429k fewer fractures). Team has established track record and collaborative MoU.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No citation metrics; publication record not explicitly detailed for the applicant; evidence is more organisational than personal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No citation metrics; publication record not explicitly detailed for the applicant; evidence is more organisational than personal.

evidence.text: 6. https: //www. i-micronews. com/products/x-ray-detectors-for-medical-industrial-and-security-applications-2019/? cn-reloaded=17. https: //www. marketsandmarkets. com/Market-Reports/diagnostic-imaging-market-411. htmlBenefit to Patients and Healthcare SystemThe software has been validated in a recent study[8]where 261 participants over 50 years old received bilateral wrist X-rays and DXA scans of their wrists, hips, and lower back. IBEX BH derived their aBMD and T-scores (whichcompare results to a healthy young population). These were compared to DXA and demonstrated: IBEX BH agrees with DXA to the same level as one DXA manufacturer agrees with another. IBEX BH is an effective screening tool with low false positive and false negative rates. IBEX BH is effective at identifying patients from a wrist X-ray who will go on to be diagnosed with osteoporosis at the hip or spine. IBEX BH is a more accurate triaging tool than the currently used NICE-recommended fracture risk prediction tool (FRAX[9])for referral to DXA or treatment.

IBEX has developed a comprehensive health economics model, audited by York Healthcare Economics Consortium. If implemented across the NHS over 20 years, IBEX BH adoption is predicted to generate up to £2bn of cost savings as a result of 429,000 fewer fractures and 12,000 fewer deaths.  Additional service user benefits include: Earlier opportunistic detection of osteoporosis in over-50s –leading to reduced time to treatment and ultimately prevention of future fragility fractures. Increased access to diagnosis for users with learning difficulties or mobility issues for whom DXA is not possible –promoting equality of service provision. Past PPI involvement has identified general support for the rationale of IBEX BH, but stressed the importance of earnt trust via an evidence base[10]. This work flagged the importance of PPIE for the successful exploitation of IBEX BH, particularly in integrating how further treatment and/or testing is communicated with patients. A PPIE lead has been identified who will actively encourage a patient voice across the project’s duration. 8. IBEX will assign a project manager to oversee delivery of the project in line with project and risk management procedures outlined in the company’s ISO13485 accredited quality management system.

Up to £10,000 is allocated to PPIE activities (travel, subsistence and time reimbursement)and research team site and conference expenses (travel, accommodation, subsistence). evidence.text: Table Of Contents1. Lead Applicant Details2. Expression of Interest1. Lead Applicant DetailsUnmet Patient NeedOsteoporosis is a debilitating disease characterised by loss of bone mass and deterioration of the bone structure,resulting in a significantly increased risk of fragility fractures. An estimated 17% of men and 34% of women over 50 are affected by osteoporosis worldwide[1], with resulting fragility fractures accounting for significant health and social care costs. In 2017, fragility fractures cost the UK £4. 5bn with an associated one-year mortality rate of 33% –and such fractures are set to rise by 27% by 2030[2]. Osteoporosis is highly treatable, but drastically under-detected. Often those at high risk of fracture are not identified or treated, with an estimated treatment gap of 66% for women in the UK[2]. IBEX Bone Health (BH)will accurately predict osteoporosis from wrist x-rays opportunistically taken using widely available equipment whenever patients are x-rayed for other indications. A data-audit at Truro showed that only 11. 8% of 2,719 wrist x-rays were referred for a costly DXA;

23% had osteoporosis. IBEX BH would ensure the other 89% were analysed at very low cost. Areal Bone Mineral Density (aBMD), measured at the hips and lumbar spine by Dual Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DXA)is the gold standard biomarker for osteoporosis. But NHS osteoporosis treatment waiting times of up to 16 months are primarily caused by poor availability of DXA equipment[4]and specialist radiographers. IBEX BH is a patented software product that derives a quantitative measure of aBMD comparable with DXA, from a standard clinical radiograph of the wrist, due to be granted CE marking imminently. IBEX BH can utilise fast, low cost and widely accessible digital radiography (DR)capacity to measure bone health and fracture risk, leading to earlier treatment and fracture prevention when used opportunistically on radiographs taken for other indications.

evidence.text: 5. Further commercial development of the software (ongoing). Applicant and Team DetailsCompany InformationIBEX is focused on the development of medical X-ray imaging software to address population health challenges andimprove the value of radiography outputs. The Company has a strong track record of success in delivering grant-funded projects, including a large clinical study funded with a €1. 6m Horizon2020 project (ref 777835)and is currently focused on commercialising the IBEX Bone Health product via integration into OEM systems. IBEX has recently completed a self-funded study in collaboration with the University of Exeter to demonstrate the accuracy of the wrist-based product, and this project builds upon that research as a stepping stone to demonstration of clinical efficacy and deployment within the healthcare setting.  Whilst this project focuses on the clinical feasibility of deploying the wrist-based IBEX BH product for digital radiography systems, other work is ongoing to develop the technology for use on additional anatomies including knee, ankle and pelvis (funded through the Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst programme)and to facilitate deployment on mammography systems to allow bone health assessments to be made from wrist scans collected on mammography at the same time as screening appointments (funded by the Innovate UK SMART programme).

IBEX employs a group of highly skilled mathematicians, physicists, product development experts and business professionals with a wide-ranging skillset that spans research and development, software engineering and product/project management and commercial engagement and exploitation. We also work with a wide network of clinicians and OEM partners meaning we are well-placed to deliver the proposed project benefits and ensure they make it into clinical practice. The project will be a blueprint to facilitate more rapid exploitation of additional IBEX BH product developments and enhancements as they come online.

IBEX will assign a project manager to oversee delivery of the project in line with project and risk management procedures outlined in the company’s ISO13485 accredited quality management system.
A financial package of up £149,492 has been costed. £22,792 will cover staff support for IBEX. £116,700 is allocated to UoE, who will be responsible for development and execution of the study. Their costs are split between staff costs and indirect and estate support. Up to £10,000 is allocated to PPIE activities (travel, subsistence and time reimbursement)and research team site and conference expenses (travel, accommodation, subsistence). The latter will be kept to a minimum with an awareness of sustainability.
External NHS Support &Treatment costs are estimated to be £4045.
i4i Connect funding will allow us to quickly validate the feasibility of suitable care pathway integrations which will culminate in the formulation of a comprehensive clinical trial design, anticipated to be supported by i4i PDA funding.
The project team has an established track record. Ibex Innovations and the University of Exeter (UoE)have worked collaboratively since 2020, in particular with successful Innovate UK funding for pre-clinical development of IBEX BH. A tripartite Memorandum of Understanding with Royal Cornwall Hospitals Trust (RCHT)has been in place since September 2023.
IBEX will provide a dedicated project lead for the duration of the project, along with technical support for the software product, and a permanently employed health statistician. IBEX also have expertise to develop the user experience and graphical user interface of IBEX BH and its outputs based on clinical and PPI feedback.
UoE will act as sponsor for the study, with Dr Robert Meertens the named principal investigator and project manager for the day-to-day running of the study. UoE has two further established academics linked to the project, with expertise in healthcare modelling (Dr Sebastian Rachuba)and health technology assessment (Zhivko Zhelev).
Dr Tom Bean (Musculoskeletal Radiologist)and Penny Lewis (DXA Service Lead)are RCHT clinical leads on the project. Funding is also requested for further clinical support of data collection from NIHR-funded research nurses, a UoE research radiographer, and research assistant from RCHT, with specific colleagues already identified for these potential roles.
Kath Maquire will act as PPIE lead for the full duration of the project, with existing links to the South West Peninsula Patient and Public Engagement Group (PenPEG)for workshop recruitment.
The project, and in particular the pilot study (work package 4)will have support from the South West NIHR Clinical Trials Unit, and project design has been supported by Imperial Research Support Service hub.
Regular monthly meetings with the aforementioned research team will be held to ensure work packages are executed to a pre-determined timeline using work packages as milestones.

evidence.text: Table Of Contents1. Lead Applicant Details2. Expression of Interest1. Lead Applicant DetailsUnmet Patient NeedOsteoporosis is a debilitating disease characterised by loss of bone mass and deterioration of the bone structure,resulting in a significantly increased risk of fragility fractures. An estimated 17% of men and 34% of women over 50 are affected by osteoporosis worldwide[1], with resulting fragility fractures accounting for significant health and social care costs. In 2017, fragility fractures cost the UK £4. 5bn with an associated one-year mortality rate of 33% –and such fractures are set to rise by 27% by 2030[2]. Osteoporosis is highly treatable, but drastically under-detected. Often those at high risk of fracture are not identified or treated, with an estimated treatment gap of 66% for women in the UK[2]. IBEX Bone Health (BH)will accurately predict osteoporosis from wrist x-rays opportunistically taken using widely available equipment whenever patients are x-rayed for other indications. A data-audit at Truro showed that only 11. 8% of 2,719 wrist x-rays were referred for a costly DXA;

23% had osteoporosis. IBEX BH would ensure the other 89% were analysed at very low cost. Areal Bone Mineral Density (aBMD), measured at the hips and lumbar spine by Dual Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DXA)is the gold standard biomarker for osteoporosis. But NHS osteoporosis treatment waiting times of up to 16 months are primarily caused by poor availability of DXA equipment[4]and specialist radiographers. IBEX BH is a patented software product that derives a quantitative measure of aBMD comparable with DXA, from a standard clinical radiograph of the wrist, due to be granted CE marking imminently. IBEX BH can utilise fast, low cost and widely accessible digital radiography (DR)capacity to measure bone health and fracture risk, leading to earlier treatment and fracture prevention when used opportunistically on radiographs taken for other indications.

evidence.text: IBEX has developed a comprehensive health economics model, audited by York Healthcare Economics Consortium. If implemented across the NHS over 20 years, IBEX BH adoption is predicted to generate up to £2bn of cost savings as a result of 429,000 fewer fractures and 12,000 fewer deaths.  Additional service user benefits include: Earlier opportunistic detection of osteoporosis in over-50s –leading to reduced time to treatment and ultimately prevention of future fragility fractures. Increased access to diagnosis for users with learning difficulties or mobility issues for whom DXA is not possible –promoting equality of service provision. Past PPI involvement has identified general support for the rationale of IBEX BH, but stressed the importance of earnt trust via an evidence base[10]. This work flagged the importance of PPIE for the successful exploitation of IBEX BH, particularly in integrating how further treatment and/or testing is communicated with patients. A PPIE lead has been identified who will actively encourage a patient voice across the project’s duration. 8. evidence.text: IBEX has developed a comprehensive health economics model, audited by York Healthcare Economics Consortium. If implemented across the NHS over 20 years, IBEX BH adoption is predicted to generate up to £2bn of cost savings as a result of 429,000 fewer fractures and 12,000 fewer deaths.  Additional service user benefits include: Earlier opportunistic detection of osteoporosis in over-50s –leading to reduced time to treatment and ultimately prevention of future fragility fractures. Increased access to diagnosis for users with learning difficulties or mobility issues for whom DXA is not possible –promoting equality of service provision. Past PPI involvement has identified general support for the rationale of IBEX BH, but stressed the importance of earnt trust via an evidence base[10]. This work flagged the importance of PPIE for the successful exploitation of IBEX BH, particularly in integrating how further treatment and/or testing is communicated with patients. A PPIE lead has been identified who will actively encourage a patient voice across the project’s duration. 8. Additional service user benefits include: Earlier opportunistic detection of osteoporosis in over-50s –leading to reduced time to treatment and ultimately prevention of future fragility fractures. Increased access to diagnosis for users with learning difficulties or mobility issues for whom DXA is not possible –promoting equality of service provision. Past PPI involvement has identified general support for the rationale of IBEX BH, but stressed the importance of earnt trust via an evidence base[10]. This work flagged the importance of PPIE for the successful exploitation of IBEX BH, particularly in integrating how further treatment and/or testing is communicated with patients. A PPIE lead has been identified who will actively encourage a patient voice across the project’s duration. 8. criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

criteria.pros: Clear budget allocation (£10k) for PPIE and a dedicated PPIE lead, demonstrating resourced inclusion plan.
criteria.drawbacks: Budget not broken down in detail; staff time for PPIE lead not explicitly costed separately.
criteria.rationale: Budget not broken down in detail; staff time for PPIE lead not explicitly costed separately.

evidence.text:
IBEX will assign a project manager to oversee delivery of the project in line with project and risk management procedures outlined in the company’s ISO13485 accredited quality management system.
A financial package of up £149,492 has been costed. £22,792 will cover staff support for IBEX. £116,700 is allocated to UoE, who will be responsible for development and execution of the study. Their costs are split between staff costs and indirect and estate support. Up to £10,000 is allocated to PPIE activities (travel, subsistence and time reimbursement)and research team site and conference expenses (travel, accommodation, subsistence). The latter will be kept to a minimum with an awareness of sustainability.
External NHS Support &Treatment costs are estimated to be £4045.
i4i Connect funding will allow us to quickly validate the feasibility of suitable care pathway integrations which will culminate in the formulation of a comprehensive clinical trial design, anticipated to be supported by i4i PDA funding.
The project team has an established track record. Ibex Innovations and the University of Exeter (UoE)have worked collaboratively since 2020, in particular with successful Innovate UK funding for pre-clinical development of IBEX BH. A tripartite Memorandum of Understanding with Royal Cornwall Hospitals Trust (RCHT)has been in place since September 2023.
IBEX will provide a dedicated project lead for the duration of the project, along with technical support for the software product, and a permanently employed health statistician. IBEX also have expertise to develop the user experience and graphical user interface of IBEX BH and its outputs based on clinical and PPI feedback.
UoE will act as sponsor for the study, with Dr Robert Meertens the named principal investigator and project manager for the day-to-day running of the study. UoE has two further established academics linked to the project, with expertise in healthcare modelling (Dr Sebastian Rachuba)and health technology assessment (Zhivko Zhelev).
Dr Tom Bean (Musculoskeletal Radiologist)and Penny Lewis (DXA Service Lead)are RCHT clinical leads on the project. Funding is also requested for further clinical support of data collection from NIHR-funded research nurses, a UoE research radiographer, and research assistant from RCHT, with specific colleagues already identified for these potential roles.
Kath Maquire will act as PPIE lead for the full duration of the project, with existing links to the South West Peninsula Patient and Public Engagement Group (PenPEG)for workshop recruitment.
The project, and in particular the pilot study (work package 4)will have support from the South West NIHR Clinical Trials Unit, and project design has been supported by Imperial Research Support Service hub.
Regular monthly meetings with the aforementioned research team will be held to ensure work packages are executed to a pre-determined timeline using work packages as milestones.
